UG-28  THICKNESS OF SHELLS AND TUBES UNDER EXTERNAL PRESSURE. (a) The required thickness of cylindrical shells under external pressure shall be determined using the procedures in this paragraph. The maximum allowable external working pressure shall not exceed the value calculated from the applicable chart, using the stress values from UG-23. (b) The thickness shall be not less than that required by UG-27 for any concurrent internal pressure condition. (c) Stiffening rings, if permitted by the design, shall be in accordance with UG-29. For design loads, see UG-22. Formed heads under external pressure shall comply with UG-33. Refer to UG-25 for corrosion provisions.
